title: melaleuca subalaris
intro: Melaleuca subalaris, also known as the narrow-leaved melaleuca or prickly paperbark, is a species of flowering plant in the myrtle family Myrtaceae. The plant is native to Western Australia and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Melaleuca subalaris include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and combat bacterial and fungal infections. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as essential oils, tinctures, and creams, which can be applied topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
